communications.subject: Formal Acknowledgement | Georgia Open Records Act Request: Georgia Revenue Dept. retention schedule
communications.communication:
Thank you for contacting the Georgia Department of Revenue. Please allow this to serve as the Department's initial response to and formal acknowledgement of your open records request.

The Department will attempt to provide all requested records, but due to confidentiality law, it is possible that some items may not be disclosed. Pursuant to O.C.G.A. § 50-18-70 et seq., the Department will provide our formal response within 7-10 business days.

Under Georgia law, an agency may impose a reasonable charge for the search, redaction, and production or copying cost of records responsive to an Open Records Request. The time and cost will be calculated by using the hourly salary of the lowest paid employee qualified to conduct the research of an Open Records Request, with no charge for the first fifteen minutes of employee time expended. DOR will provide an estimate to the requestor if the reasonably anticipated costs for Production Tasks exceed $25 and will defer the Production Tasks until the requestor agrees to pay estimated costs.

title: Obama Presidential Library: Saudi Investment in Twitter Inc. and National Security Implications (2011–2017)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Obama Presidential Library: Saudi Investment in Twitter Inc. and National Security Implications (2011–2017)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Aaccess to and copies of all records, including but not limited to memoranda, email correspondence, PowerPoint presentations, audio recordings, video recordings, briefing papers, and situation reports, created or received by the Executive Office of the President (EOP) between January 1, 2011, and January 20, 2017, regarding the ownership of Twitter Inc. by Saudi nationals and entities.

Scope of Request:
I am specifically seeking records from the National Security Council (NSC), the National Economic Council (NEC), and the Office of the Vice President that discuss:

The 2011 initial investment and the October 2015 increase in shareholding (to approximately 5.2%) in Twitter Inc. by Prince Alwaleed bin Talal and/or the Kingdom Holding Company (KHC).

Any inter-agency assessments regarding the national security implications of Saudi "passive" investment in U.S. social media platforms, specifically concerning the protection of U.S. person data and the potential for foreign surveillance.

Communications or briefings regarding the FBI’s 2015 notification to Twitter Inc. regarding the infiltration of the company by agents acting on behalf of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia (e.g., the Ali Alzabarah and Ahmad Abouammo cases).

Records discussing the transition of influence or assets from Prince Alwaleed bin Talal to Mohammad bin Salman (MBS) or the Public Investment Fund (PIF) as it relates to U.S. technology firms.

Statutory Acknowledgment regarding Section 721(c):

I recognize that Section 721(c) of the Defense Production Act (50 U.S.C. § 4565) provides a statutory exemption for information filed with CFIUS. However, this request specifically excludes the raw 'notice' or 'declaration' filed by the private parties. Instead, it seeks the derivative executive branch records—such as policy memoranda, NSC/NEC briefing slides, and inter-agency correspondence—that discuss the national security implications of the social media and telecommunications industry and the impact of Saudi sovereign investment on U.S. digital infrastructure. I request that all reasonably segregable, non-exempt portions of these records be released.

Please provide any reasonable segregable portion of a requested record after removing or redacting those portions claimed to be exempt, explain in writing the justification for redacting the record, indicate the extent of any redactions on the portion of the record which is made available or published and where technically feasible indicate on the redacted portion itself the specific exemption(s) claimed.

Search Keywords:

"Twitter", "Alwaleed bin Talal", "Kingdom Holding Company", "KHC", "Mohammad bin Salman", "MBS", "Public Investment Fund", "PIF", "CFIUS", "Spy Ring", "Alzabarah", "Abouammo", "Saudi Surveillance", "Data Privacy", "Social Media Foreign Ownership".

Thank you to the Barack Obama Presidential Library and for the library's commitment to transparency.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware

title: Obama Presidential Library: CFIUS Review of Aditya Birla Group / Columbian Chemicals (2010–2012)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Obama Presidential Library: CFIUS Review of Aditya Birla Group / Columbian Chemicals (2010–2012)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Access to and copies of all records created or received by the Executive Office of the President (EOP) between January 1, 2010, and December 31, 2012, regarding the Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States (CFIUS) national security review of the acquisition of Columbian Chemicals by the Aditya Birla Group (including its subsidiaries Indigold Carbon, Alexandria Carbon Black, or Thai Carbon Black).

For additional context:

https://www.k-online.com/en/Media_News/News/K_Archive/Birla_Carbon_to_acquire_Columbian_Chemicals

Scope of Request:

This is a request for the government's "derivative work" related to a CFIUS review of the Columbian Chemical acquisition by Aditya Birla. I am seeking high-level briefings, memoranda, situation room reports, PowerPoint Presentations, audio and/or video recordings and email correspondence within the National Security Council (NSC), the National Economic Council (NEC), and the Office of the Vice President that discuss:

The strategic importance of the carbon black supply chain to the U.S. automotive and defense industries.

Potential national security risks associated with the transfer of Columbian Chemicals’ proprietary R&D and manufacturing assets to a foreign conglomerate.

The "Industrial Security" implications of the 2011 acquisition, specifically any concerns regarding the conglomerate's global footprint or ties to other foreign powers.

For the purposes of redaction: I recognize that Section 721(c) of the Defense Production Act (50 U.S.C. § 4565) provides a statutory exemption for information filed with CFIUS. However, this request specifically excludes the raw 'notice' or 'declaration' filed by the private parties. Instead, it seeks the derivative executive branch records—such as policy memoranda, NSC briefing slides, and inter-agency correspondence—that discuss the national security implications of the carbon black industry and the Aditya Birla Group’s entry into the U.S. market. I request that all reasonably segregable, non-exempt portions of these records be released. In plain language this request asks for line-by-line evidence of what is being redacted. 

Search Keywords:

To facilitate your search, please include the following keywords in your query of the Electronic Records Archive (ERA):

"Columbian Chemicals", "Aditya Birla", "Birla Carbon", "Indigold Carbon", "CFIUS", "Carbon Black", "Kumar Mangalam Birla", "One Equity Partners", "JPMorgan Chase", "Centerville LA", "Marietta GA", "Dr. Santrupt Misra"

Thank you to the Obama Library's commitment to transparency. I look forward to working with you in resolving this request.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware

title: Face recognition agreements, training, and use materials

communications.subject: Arizona Public Records Law Request: Face recognition agreements, training, and use materials
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Arizona Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

I hereby request the following records related to this agency’s use of any face recognition platform, including but not limited to any system over which this agency does not maintain primary ownership but to which members of the agency have access or any platform that enables face recognition:

1. AGREEMENTS and CONTRACTS
Any and all agreements (including related amendments, attachments, and exhibits) related to the acquisition and use of this "software," algorithm, or program.

2. INSTRUCTIONAL MATERIALS and TRAINING
All instructional materials, presentations and presentation materials and other guidance on the use of "the software." Responsive materials include recorded video and audio, PowerPoint files, prepared remarks, and slides. This also includes training material governing the use, sharing, or access to the "software" or any data related to or collected by the face recognition software/technology, including the legal standard that is required before using the technology.

3. PRIVACY ASSESSMENTS
A copy of any data retention guidelines or policies or other materials evaluating or guiding the security of the "software" or the privacy of the data involved.

4. USE POLICIES
Please provide a copy of any policy directives, guidance documents, memoranda, training materials, or similar records governing the use and function of this technology for any law enforcement purpose. Responsive materials include all those related to data retention, permissible and impermissible use, and security standards.

5. USE LOGS
All logs documenting the use of and searched conducted using the face recognition system for the period from August 1, 2025 through February 23, 2026.

communications.subject: FOIA Received 2280
communications.communication:
Greetings,

The Maricopa County Sheriff’s Office has received your request for public records and is processing your request. All requests are processed in the order in which they are received, and no requests are prioritized by deadline date. The amount of time to completely process your request depends upon the number of pending requests, volume of material encompassed in the request, and the amount of redaction, if any, that is necessary.

Please be aware, if you are requesting records with large amount of body camera videos and or high-profile cases, the time to complete your request may be significantly longer as these cases are often voluminous. Identifying the specific record(s) being requested may help expedite your request. You will be notified once records are made available for you to download or pick up, or if no records are found. Your tracking # is 2280. When checking the status of your request, please send emails to mediarequest@mcso.maricopa.gov<mailto:mediarequest@mcso.maricopa.gov> and include your tracking #.

***Pursuant to ARS 39-129, the Maricopa County Sheriff’s Office (MCSO) Legal Liaison Section has implemented a fee schedule for requests for video recordings.  The fee for processing video recordings is $25 per each video-hour ($6.25 per quarter hour).  In the event there are contemporaneous multiple requests for the same video, the cost associated with processing the video will be shared equally between all requesting parties at that time. Requests for previously processed videos will be at no cost unless the requestor wishes to receive a hard copy instead of an electronic copy. Fees for hardcopies are listed below. ***

The fee breakdown for hard copies are as follows:

Documents:                       $.50 for each page

CD’s:                                      $15.00 each

Thank you

—
Calbert Gillett
[Sent from Front]

title: Port Allen ICE detention communication (Mayor's Office)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Louisiana Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Under the Louisiana Open Records Act, I am requesting an opportunity to obtain copies of public records relating to the property located at 2070 Commercial Dr, Port Allen, LA 70767.
Specifically, I am requesting the following records created, submitted, or received between January 1, 2025 and the date this request is processed:

All emails, text messages, memos, and calendar appointments sent or received by [Insert Specific Official's Name or "the Mayor's Office"] containing any of the following keywords: "ICE", "Immigration and Customs Enforcement", "DHS", "Homeland Security", "detention", "processing center", or "[Insert specific LLC name or address]".
Minutes, transcripts, agendas, and notes from any open, closed-door, or executive sessions where the sale, lease, or zoning of the aforementioned property was discussed.

To expedite this request and reduce the administrative burden on your office, I am requesting these records in their native electronic format (e.g., PDF for documents, .PST or .EML for emails) delivered via email or secure download link.
If any part of this request is denied or redacted, please provide a written response citing the specific statutory exemption being applied.
If there are any fees for searching or copying these records, please inform me beforehand if the estimated cost will exceed $25. However, I also request a waiver of all fees, as the disclosure of this information is in the public interest and will contribute significantly to the public’s understanding of local government operations and infrastructure planning.
Thank you for your prompt assistance and public service.
Sincerely,

Ricky Harrershow

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Ricky Harrershow
